31 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:金刺盛澄 up to rev 100704308
revision.text:
[[ファイル:Suwa Morizumi.jpg|thumb|250px|諏訪盛澄（金刺盛澄）／[[江戸時代]]『[[前賢故実]]』より。[[菊池容斎]]画]] '''金刺 盛澄'''（かなさし の もりずみ、生没年未詳）は、[[平安時代]]後期の[[諏訪大社]][[諏訪大社#下社|下社]]の[[神官]]（[[諏訪大社#神職|大祝]]）・[[武士]]<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/1000/100115.htm なんでも諏訪百科『金刺盛澄』] - [[諏訪市博物館]]</ref>。'''諏訪盛澄'''とも呼ばれる<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/gojuuon/13_su/~su_69.htm なんでも諏訪百科『諏訪盛澄』諏訪市博物館]</ref>。弟に[[手塚光盛]]がいる。
== 生涯 == 当初は[[治承・寿永の乱]]での[[源義仲]]の挙兵に従ったが、御射山神事のため弟の光盛を留め置いて帰国した。[[伊勢平氏|平家]]の家人として長年在京していたことから（『[[吾妻鏡]]』[[文治]]3年8月15日条）、義仲の討伐後、[[源頼朝]]によって捕縛され、[[梶原景時]]に預けられた。頼朝は盛澄を[[処刑]]しようとしていたが、盛澄が[[藤原秀郷]]流[[弓術]]を継承する名手であったことから、景時は盛澄の命を奪うのを惜しみ、頼朝に説得を重ねた末、せめて盛澄の弓の技量を見てから死罪にして欲しい、と請願する。
盛澄は頼朝の基に参上し、[[鶴岡八幡宮]]での[[放生会]]で[[流鏑馬]]を披露した。この時頼朝は盛澄が騎乗する[[ウマ|馬]]としてわざと暴れ馬を与えた上、盛澄が指定された八つの的を射抜くと、射抜いた的の破片、さらに的を立てかけた串を射抜くよう難題を押し付けてきたが、盛澄は見事に全て射抜いたため、赦免された。この時、景時が同じく捕縛された義仲の郎党達にも寛恕を施して欲しい、と頼朝に願い出て、その郎党達もまた助命され、[[御家人]]に列した<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 『諏訪盛澄』] - [[コトバンク]]</ref>（『[[吾妻鏡]]』文治3年8月15日条）。これらの顛末は『[[諏方大明神画詞|諏訪大明神絵詞]]』でも描写されている。
[[建久]]4年（1193年）3月、頼朝が[[下野国]][[那須]]、[[上野国]]三原で行った[[狩倉]]にも随伴し、以後は幕府の流鏑馬や的始の儀式で活躍した。同年9月、[[北条泰時]]の元服前の「[[矢開き|矢口餅]]の儀式」では三の口を賜っている（『[[吾妻鏡]]』建久4年9月11日条）。史料では[[建仁]]3年（[[1203年]]）までの活動が見られる。[[下諏訪町]]には盛澄が恩人の景時を偲び建立した梶原[[塚]]がある。
== 画像集 == <gallery widths="200px" heights="140px"> 梶原塚（下諏訪町）.jpg|梶原塚（下諏訪町）（[[長野県]][[諏訪郡]]下諏訪町木の下） 梶原塚全景.jpg|梶原塚全景(右へ諏訪大社下宮[[諏訪大社#秋宮|秋宮]]) </gallery>
== 参考文献 == *『長野県百科事典』[[信濃毎日新聞]]社 *『日本中世史事典』[[朝倉書店]]
== 関連項目 == *[[諏訪神党]] *[[諏訪氏]] *[[山吹御前]] *折井宏光 - 歴史画家、金刺盛澄を描いた<ref>[http://suwagunshihaku.org/article/56094157.html 諏訪湖博物館 特別展「金刺盛澄を描く～折井宏光挿絵原画展～」（2012年） - 諏訪郡市博物館等連絡協議会]</ref>
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かなさし の もりすみ}} [[Category:金刺氏|もりすみ]] [[Category:諏訪氏|もりすみ]] [[Category:平安時代の武士]] [[Category:平安時代の神職]] [[Category:鎌倉幕府御家人]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の神職]] [[Category:源義仲]] [[Category:治承・寿永の乱の人物]] [[Category:12世紀生]] [[Category:13世紀没]]

{{Infobox person | image = Suwa Morizumi.jpg | caption = Kanasa Mori-sumi (諏訪盛澄 / 金刺盛澄), depicted in the Edo-period work ''Zenkō Kojitsu'' by {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|ja|菊池容斎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | occupation = Priest of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Lower Shrine, Samurai }}
'''Kanasa Mori-sumi''' (金刺 盛澄, also known as Suwa Mori-sumi) was a priest ({{ill|Ōhō|ja|大祝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and samurai of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Lower Shrine during the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/1000/100115.htm 諏訪市博物館 - ''Suwa City Museum: Kanasa Mori-sumi'']</ref> He is also referred to as {{ill|Suwa Mori-sumi|ja|諏訪盛澄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/gojuuon/13_su/~su_69.htm ''Suwa City Museum: Suwa Mori-sumi'']</ref> His younger brother was {{ill|Tezuka Mitsumori|ja|手塚光盛|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
## Biography Initially, Kanasa Mori-sumi joined {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|ja|源義仲|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Genpei War|ja|治承・寿永の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}} but later returned to his homeland to oversee the {{ill|Misayama Ritual|ja|御射山神事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and left his younger brother Mitsumori in his stead. Due to his longstanding affiliation with the {{ill|Heike|ja|平家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, recorded in the ''{{ill|Azuma Kagami|ja|吾妻鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' under the entry for {{ill|Bunji|ja|文治|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (1187), August 15, Mori-sumi was captured by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and placed under the custody of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|ja|梶原景時|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
Yoritomo initially intended to execute Mori-sumi. However, Kagetoki, recognizing Mori-sumi's skill in {{ill|archery|ja|弓術|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, persuaded Yoritomo to allow Mori-sumi to demonstrate his abilities before his execution. At the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Hōjōe|ja|放生会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of {{ill|yabusame|ja|流鏑馬|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became {{ill|gokenin|ja|御家人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day {{ill|Shimosuwa|ja|下諏訪町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.
## Gallery <gallery widths="200px" heights="140px"> 梶原塚（下諏訪町）.jpg|Kajiwara Zuka (Shimosuwa Town), {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 梶原塚全景.jpg|View of Kajiwara Zuka near Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya </gallery>
## References * ''Nagano Encyclopedia'' (長野県百科事典), published by {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * ''Dictionary of Medieval Japanese History'' (日本中世史事典), published by {{ill|Asakura Shoten|ja|朝倉書店|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
## See also * {{ill|Suwa Shinbō|ja|諏訪神党|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Suwa Clan|ja|諏訪氏|1=Suwa clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Gozen|ja|山吹御前|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Orihiromitsu|ja|折井宏光|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Historical painter who depicted Kanasa Mori-sumi.<ref>[http://suwagunshihaku.org/article/56094157.html 諏訪湖博物館特別展: "Drawing Kanasa Mori-sumi ~ Orihiromitsu Exhibition" (2012)]</ref>

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:平安時代の神職
revision.text:
{{Infobox person | image = Suwa Morizumi.jpg | caption = Kanasa Mori-sumi (諏訪盛澄 / 金刺盛澄), depicted in the Edo-period work ''Zenkō Kojitsu'' by [[:en:Kikuchi Yōsai|Kikuchi Yōsai]]. | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | occupation = Priest of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine, Samurai }}
'''Kanasa Mori-sumi''' (金刺 盛澄, also known as Suwa Mori-sumi) was a priest ({{ill|Ōhō|ja|大祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō|qq=}}) and samurai of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/1000/100115.htm 諏訪市博物館 - ''Suwa City Museum: Kanasa Mori-sumi'']</ref> He is also referred to as {{ill|Suwa Mori-sumi|ja|諏訪盛澄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Mori-sumi|qq=}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/gojuuon/13_su/~su_69.htm ''Suwa City Museum: Suwa Mori-sumi'']</ref> His younger brother was {{ill|Tezuka Mitsumori|ja|手塚光盛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tezuka Mitsumori|qq=}}.
## Biography Initially, Kanasa Mori-sumi joined [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] during the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] but later returned to his homeland to oversee the {{ill|Misayama Ritual|ja|御射山神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Ritual|qq=}} and left his younger brother Mitsumori in his stead. Due to his longstanding affiliation with the {{ill|Heike|ja|平家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heike|qq=}}, recorded in the ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' under the entry for [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 3 (1187), August 15, Mori-sumi was captured by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and placed under the custody of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]].
Yoritomo initially intended to execute Mori-sumi. However, Kagetoki, recognizing Mori-sumi's skill in [[:en:Kyūjutsu|archery]], persuaded Yoritomo to allow Mori-sumi to demonstrate his abilities before his execution. At the [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] during the [[:en:Life release|Hōjōe]], Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of [[:en:Yabusame|yabusame]], accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became [[:en:Gokenin|gokenin]].<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.
## Gallery <gallery widths="200px" heights="140px"> 梶原塚（下諏訪町）.jpg|Kajiwara Zuka (Shimosuwa Town), [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] 梶原塚全景.jpg|View of Kajiwara Zuka near Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya </gallery>

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:鎌倉幕府御家人
revision.text:
{{Infobox person | image = Suwa Morizumi.jpg | caption = Kanasa Mori-sumi (諏訪盛澄 / 金刺盛澄), depicted in the Edo-period work ''Zenkō Kojitsu'' by [[:en:Kikuchi Yōsai|Kikuchi Yōsai]]. | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | occupation = Priest of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine, Samurai }}
'''Kanasa Mori-sumi''' (金刺 盛澄, also known as Suwa Mori-sumi) was a priest ({{ill|Ōhō|ja|大祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō|qq=}}) and samurai of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/1000/100115.htm 諏訪市博物館 - ''Suwa City Museum: Kanasa Mori-sumi'']</ref> He is also referred to as {{ill|Suwa Mori-sumi|ja|諏訪盛澄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Mori-sumi|qq=}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/gojuuon/13_su/~su_69.htm ''Suwa City Museum: Suwa Mori-sumi'']</ref> His younger brother was {{ill|Tezuka Mitsumori|ja|手塚光盛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tezuka Mitsumori|qq=}}.
## Biography Initially, Kanasa Mori-sumi joined [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] during the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] but later returned to his homeland to oversee the {{ill|Misayama Ritual|ja|御射山神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Ritual|qq=}} and left his younger brother Mitsumori in his stead. Due to his longstanding affiliation with the {{ill|Heike|ja|平家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heike|qq=}}, recorded in the ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' under the entry for [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 3 (1187), August 15, Mori-sumi was captured by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and placed under the custody of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]].
Yoritomo initially intended to execute Mori-sumi. However, Kagetoki, recognizing Mori-sumi's skill in [[:en:Kyūjutsu|archery]], persuaded Yoritomo to allow Mori-sumi to demonstrate his abilities before his execution. At the [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] during the [[:en:Life release|Hōjōe]], Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of [[:en:Yabusame|yabusame]], accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became [[:en:Gokenin|gokenin]].<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.
## Gallery <gallery widths="200px" heights="140px"> 梶原塚（下諏訪町）.jpg|Kajiwara Zuka (Shimosuwa Town), [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] 梶原塚全景.jpg|View of Kajiwara Zuka near Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya </gallery>

<!--Kana Moritomo--><!--Kana Moritomo--> [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:平安時代の神職]] [[Category:People of the Genpei War]] [[Category:源義仲]] [[Category:金刺氏]] [[Category:Gokenin of the Kamakura shogunate]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の神職]]

{{Infobox person | image = Suwa Morizumi.jpg | caption = Kanasa Mori-sumi (諏訪盛澄 / 金刺盛澄), depicted in the Edo-period work ''Zenkō Kojitsu'' by [[:en:Kikuchi Yōsai|Kikuchi Yōsai]]. | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | occupation = Priest of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine, Samurai }}
'''Kanasa Mori-sumi''' (金刺 盛澄, also known as Suwa Mori-sumi) was a priest ({{ill|Ōhō|ja|大祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō|qq=}}) and samurai of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/1000/100115.htm 諏訪市博物館 - ''Suwa City Museum: Kanasa Mori-sumi'']</ref> He is also referred to as {{ill|Suwa Mori-sumi|ja|諏訪盛澄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Mori-sumi|qq=}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/gojuuon/13_su/~su_69.htm ''Suwa City Museum: Suwa Mori-sumi'']</ref> His younger brother was {{ill|Tezuka Mitsumori|ja|手塚光盛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tezuka Mitsumori|qq=}}.
## Biography Initially, Kanasa Mori-sumi joined [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] during the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] but later returned to his homeland to oversee the {{ill|Misayama Ritual|ja|御射山神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Ritual|qq=}} and left his younger brother Mitsumori in his stead. Due to his longstanding affiliation with the {{ill|Heike|ja|平家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heike|qq=}}, recorded in the ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' under the entry for [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 3 (1187), August 15, Mori-sumi was captured by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and placed under the custody of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]].
Yoritomo initially intended to execute Mori-sumi. However, Kagetoki, recognizing Mori-sumi's skill in [[:en:Kyūjutsu|archery]], persuaded Yoritomo to allow Mori-sumi to demonstrate his abilities before his execution. At the [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] during the [[:en:Life release|Hōjōe]], Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of [[:en:Yabusame|yabusame]], accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became [[:en:Gokenin|gokenin]].<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.
## Gallery <gallery widths="200px" heights="140px"> 梶原塚（下諏訪町）.jpg|Kajiwara Zuka (Shimosuwa Town), [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] 梶原塚全景.jpg|View of Kajiwara Zuka near Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya </gallery>

<!--Kana Moritomo--><!--Kana Moritomo--> [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:平安時代の神職]] [[Category:People of the Genpei War]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] [[Category:金刺氏]] [[Category:Gokenin of the Kamakura shogunate]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の神職]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

{{Infobox person | image = Suwa Morizumi.jpg | caption = Kanasa Mori-sumi (諏訪盛澄 / 金刺盛澄), depicted in the Edo-period work ''Zenkō Kojitsu'' by [[:en:Kikuchi Yōsai|Kikuchi Yōsai]]. | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | occupation = Priest of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine, Samurai }}
'''Kanasa Mori-sumi''' (金刺 盛澄, also known as Suwa Mori-sumi) was a priest ({{ill|Ōhō|ja|大祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}}) and samurai of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/1000/100115.htm 諏訪市博物館 - ''Suwa City Museum: Kanasa Mori-sumi'']</ref> He is also referred to as {{ill|Suwa Mori-sumi|ja|諏訪盛澄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Mori-sumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺盛澄}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/gojuuon/13_su/~su_69.htm ''Suwa City Museum: Suwa Mori-sumi'']</ref> His younger brother was {{ill|Tezuka Mitsumori|ja|手塚光盛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tezuka Mitsumori|qq=}}.
## Biography Initially, Kanasa Mori-sumi joined [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] during the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] but later returned to his homeland to oversee the {{ill|Misayama Ritual|ja|御射山神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and left his younger brother Mitsumori in his stead. Due to his longstanding affiliation with the {{ill|Heike|ja|平家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢平氏}}, recorded in the ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' under the entry for [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 3 (1187), August 15, Mori-sumi was captured by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and placed under the custody of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]].
Yoritomo initially intended to execute Mori-sumi. However, Kagetoki, recognizing Mori-sumi's skill in [[:en:Kyūjutsu|archery]], persuaded Yoritomo to allow Mori-sumi to demonstrate his abilities before his execution. At the [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] during the [[:en:Life release|Hōjōe]], Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of [[:en:Yabusame|yabusame]], accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became [[:en:Gokenin|gokenin]].<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.

Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became [[:en:Gokenin|gokenin]].<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Kamakura gokenin
revision.text:
{{Infobox person | image = Suwa Morizumi.jpg | caption = Kanasa Mori-sumi (諏訪盛澄 / 金刺盛澄), depicted in the Edo-period work ''Zenkō Kojitsu'' by [[:en:Kikuchi Yōsai|Kikuchi Yōsai]]. | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | occupation = Priest of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine, Samurai }}
'''Kanasa Mori-sumi''' (金刺 盛澄, also known as Suwa Mori-sumi) was a priest ({{ill|Ōhō|ja|大祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}}) and samurai of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/1000/100115.htm 諏訪市博物館 - ''Suwa City Museum: Kanasa Mori-sumi'']</ref> He is also referred to as {{ill|Suwa Mori-sumi|ja|諏訪盛澄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Mori-sumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺盛澄}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/gojuuon/13_su/~su_69.htm ''Suwa City Museum: Suwa Mori-sumi'']</ref> His younger brother was {{ill|Tezuka Mitsumori|ja|手塚光盛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tezuka Mitsumori|qq=}}.
## Biography Initially, Kanasa Mori-sumi joined [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] during the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] but later returned to his homeland to oversee the {{ill|Misayama Ritual|ja|御射山神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and left his younger brother Mitsumori in his stead. Due to his longstanding affiliation with the {{ill|Heike|ja|平家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢平氏}}, recorded in the ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' under the entry for [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 3 (1187), August 15, Mori-sumi was captured by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and placed under the custody of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]].
Yoritomo initially intended to execute Mori-sumi. However, Kagetoki, recognizing Mori-sumi's skill in [[:en:Kyūjutsu|archery]], persuaded Yoritomo to allow Mori-sumi to demonstrate his abilities before his execution. At the [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] during the [[:en:Life release|Hōjōe]], Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of [[:en:Yabusame|yabusame]], accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became [[:en:Gokenin|gokenin]].<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.

At the [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] during the [[:en:Life release|Hōjōe]], Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of [[:en:Yabusame|yabusame]], accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became [[:en:Gokenin|gokenin]].<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.

{{Infobox person | image = Suwa Morizumi.jpg | caption = Kanasa Mori-sumi (諏訪盛澄 / 金刺盛澄), depicted in the Edo-period work ''Zenkō Kojitsu'' by [[:en:Kikuchi Yōsai|Kikuchi Yōsai]]. | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | occupation = Priest of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine, Samurai }}
'''Kanasa Mori-sumi''' (金刺 盛澄, also known as Suwa Mori-sumi) was a priest ({{ill|Ōhō|ja|大祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}}) and samurai of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/1000/100115.htm 諏訪市博物館 - ''Suwa City Museum: Kanasa Mori-sumi'']</ref> He is also referred to as {{ill|Suwa Mori-sumi|ja|諏訪盛澄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Mori-sumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺盛澄}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/gojuuon/13_su/~su_69.htm ''Suwa City Museum: Suwa Mori-sumi'']</ref> His younger brother was {{ill|Tezuka Mitsumori|ja|手塚光盛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tezuka Mitsumori|qq=}}.
## Biography Initially, Kanasa Mori-sumi joined [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] during the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] but later returned to his homeland to oversee the {{ill|Misayama Ritual|ja|御射山神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and left his younger brother Mitsumori in his stead. Due to his longstanding affiliation with the {{ill|Heike|ja|平家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢平氏}}, recorded in the ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' under the entry for [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 3 (1187), August 15, Mori-sumi was captured by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and placed under the custody of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]].
Yoritomo initially intended to execute Mori-sumi. However, Kagetoki, recognizing Mori-sumi's skill in [[:en:Kyūjutsu|archery]], persuaded Yoritomo to allow Mori-sumi to demonstrate his abilities before his execution. At the [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] during the [[:en:Life release|Hōjōe]], Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of [[:en:Yabusame|yabusame]], accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became [[:en:Gokenin|gokenin]].<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.

At the [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] during the [[:en:Life release|Hōjōe]], Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of [[:en:Yabusame|yabusame]], accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became [[:en:Gokenin|gokenin]].<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.

revision.text:
{{Infobox person | image = Suwa Morizumi.jpg | caption = Kanasa Mori-sumi (諏訪盛澄 / 金刺盛澄), depicted in the Edo-period work ''Zenkō Kojitsu'' by [[:en:Kikuchi Yōsai|Kikuchi Yōsai]]. | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | occupation = Priest of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine, Samurai }}
'''Kanasa Mori-sumi''' (金刺 盛澄, also known as Suwa Mori-sumi) was a priest ({{ill|Ōhō|ja|大祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}}) and samurai of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/1000/100115.htm 諏訪市博物館 - ''Suwa City Museum: Kanasa Mori-sumi'']</ref> He is also referred to as {{ill|Suwa Mori-sumi|ja|諏訪盛澄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Mori-sumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺盛澄}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/gojuuon/13_su/~su_69.htm ''Suwa City Museum: Suwa Mori-sumi'']</ref> His younger brother was {{ill|Tezuka Mitsumori|ja|手塚光盛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tezuka Mitsumori|qq=}}.
## Biography Initially, Kanasa Mori-sumi joined [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] during the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] but later returned to his homeland to oversee the {{ill|Misayama Ritual|ja|御射山神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and left his younger brother Mitsumori in his stead. Due to his longstanding affiliation with the {{ill|Heike|ja|平家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢平氏}}, recorded in the ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' under the entry for [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 3 (1187), August 15, Mori-sumi was captured by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and placed under the custody of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]].
Yoritomo initially intended to execute Mori-sumi. However, Kagetoki, recognizing Mori-sumi's skill in [[:en:Kyūjutsu|archery]], persuaded Yoritomo to allow Mori-sumi to demonstrate his abilities before his execution. At the [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] during the [[:en:Life release|Hōjōe]], Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of [[:en:Yabusame|yabusame]], accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became [[:en:Gokenin|gokenin]].<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.
## Gallery <gallery widths="200px" heights="140px"> 梶原塚（下諏訪町）.jpg|Kajiwara Zuka (Shimosuwa Town), [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] 梶原塚全景.jpg|View of Kajiwara Zuka near Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya </gallery>

'''Kanasa Mori-sumi''' (金刺 盛澄, also known as Suwa Mori-sumi) was a priest ({{ill|Ōhō|ja|大祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)}}) and samurai of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/1000/100115.htm 諏訪市博物館 - ''Suwa City Museum: Kanasa Mori-sumi'']</ref> He is also referred to as {{ill|Suwa Mori-sumi|ja|諏訪盛澄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Mori-sumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺盛澄}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/gojuuon/13_su/~su_69.htm ''Suwa City Museum: Suwa Mori-sumi'']</ref> His younger brother was {{ill|Tezuka Mitsumori|ja|手塚光盛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tezuka Mitsumori|qq=}}.
## Biography Initially, Kanasa Mori-sumi joined [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] during the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] but later returned to his homeland to oversee the {{ill|Misayama Ritual|ja|御射山神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and left his younger brother Mitsumori in his stead. Due to his longstanding affiliation with the {{ill|Heike|ja|平家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢平氏}}, recorded in the ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' under the entry for [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 3 (1187), August 15, Mori-sumi was captured by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and placed under the custody of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]].
Yoritomo initially intended to execute Mori-sumi. However, Kagetoki, recognizing Mori-sumi's skill in [[:en:Kyūjutsu|archery]], persuaded Yoritomo to allow Mori-sumi to demonstrate his abilities before his execution. At the [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] during the [[:en:Life release|Hōjōe]], Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of [[:en:Yabusame|yabusame]], accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became [[:en:Gokenin|gokenin]].<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.
## Gallery <gallery widths="200px" heights="140px"> 梶原塚（下諏訪町）.jpg|Kajiwara Zuka (Shimosuwa Town), [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] 梶原塚全景.jpg|View of Kajiwara Zuka near Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya </gallery>

Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became [[:en:Gokenin|gokenin]].<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刺盛澄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646497]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646497]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox person | image = Suwa Morizumi.jpg | caption = Kanasa Mori-sumi (諏訪盛澄 / 金刺盛澄), depicted in the Edo-period work ''Zenkō Kojitsu'' by [[:en:Kikuchi Yōsai|Kikuchi Yōsai]]. | birth_date = 1150 | death_date = Unknown | occupation = {{ill|bushi|WD=Q1975935}} | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | children = {{ill|Yamabuki Gozen|WD=Q11467654}} | father = {{ill|Q106616045|WD=Q106616045}} }}
'''Kanasa Mori-sumi''' (金刺 盛澄, also known as Suwa Mori-sumi) was a priest ({{ill|Ōhō|ja|大祝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|1=Hōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869239}}) and samurai of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/1000/100115.htm 諏訪市博物館 - ''Suwa City Museum: Kanasa Mori-sumi'']</ref> He is also referred to as {{ill|Suwa Mori-sumi|ja|諏訪盛澄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Mori-sumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺盛澄|1=Kana Moritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646497}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/gojuuon/13_su/~su_69.htm ''Suwa City Museum: Suwa Mori-sumi'']</ref> His younger brother was {{ill|Tezuka Mitsumori|ja|手塚光盛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tezuka Mitsumori|qq=}}.
## Biography Initially, Kanasa Mori-sumi joined [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] during the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] but later returned to his homeland to oversee the {{ill|Misayama Ritual|ja|御射山神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Ritual|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and left his younger brother Mitsumori in his stead. Due to his longstanding affiliation with the {{ill|Heike|ja|平家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heike|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢平氏|1=Ise Heishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15734686}}, recorded in the ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' under the entry for [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 3 (1187), August 15, Mori-sumi was captured by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and placed under the custody of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]].
Yoritomo initially intended to execute Mori-sumi. However, Kagetoki, recognizing Mori-sumi's skill in [[:en:Kyūjutsu|archery]], persuaded Yoritomo to allow Mori-sumi to demonstrate his abilities before his execution. At the [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] during the [[:en:Life release|Hōjōe]], Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of [[:en:Yabusame|yabusame]], accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became [[:en:Gokenin|gokenin]].<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.

<!--Kana Moritomo--><!--Kana Moritomo--> [[Category:Samurai of Heian period]] [[Category:Shinto priests of the Heian period]] [[Category:People of the Genpei War]] [[Category:Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] [[Category:Kanasashi Clan]] [[Category:Gokenin of the Kamakura shogunate]] [[Category:Shinto priests of the Kamakura period]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11646497}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刺盛澄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11646497]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11646497]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox person | image = Suwa Morizumi.jpg | caption = Kanasa Mori-sumi (諏訪盛澄 / 金刺盛澄), depicted in the Edo-period work ''Zenkō Kojitsu'' by [[:en:Kikuchi Yōsai|Kikuchi Yōsai]]. | birth_date = 1150 | death_date = Unknown | occupation = {{ill|bushi|WD=Q1975935}} | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | children = {{ill|Yamabuki Gozen|WD=Q11467654}} | father = {{ill|Q106616045|WD=Q106616045}} }}
'''Kanasa Mori-sumi''' (金刺 盛澄, also known as Suwa Mori-sumi) was a priest ({{ill|Ōhō|ja|大祝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|1=Hōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869239}}) and samurai of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/1000/100115.htm 諏訪市博物館 - ''Suwa City Museum: Kanasa Mori-sumi'']</ref> He is also referred to as {{ill|Suwa Mori-sumi|ja|諏訪盛澄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Mori-sumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺盛澄|1=Kana Moritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646497}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/gojuuon/13_su/~su_69.htm ''Suwa City Museum: Suwa Mori-sumi'']</ref> His younger brother was {{ill|Tezuka Mitsumori|ja|手塚光盛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tezuka Mitsumori|qq=}}.
## Biography Initially, Kanasa Mori-sumi joined [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] during the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] but later returned to his homeland to oversee the {{ill|Misayama Ritual|ja|御射山神事|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and left his younger brother Mitsumori in his stead. Due to his longstanding affiliation with the {{ill|Heike|ja|平家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heike|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢平氏|1=Ise Heishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15734686}}, recorded in the ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' under the entry for [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 3 (1187), August 15, Mori-sumi was captured by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and placed under the custody of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]].
Yoritomo initially intended to execute Mori-sumi. However, Kagetoki, recognizing Mori-sumi's skill in [[:en:Kyūjutsu|archery]], persuaded Yoritomo to allow Mori-sumi to demonstrate his abilities before his execution. At the [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] during the [[:en:Life release|Hōjōe]], Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of [[:en:Yabusame|yabusame]], accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became [[:en:Gokenin|gokenin]].<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.

Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became [[:en:Gokenin|gokenin]].<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.

'''Kanasa Mori-sumi''' (金刺 盛澄, also known as Suwa Mori-sumi) was a priest ({{ill|Ōhō|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|1=Hōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869239}}) and samurai of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/1000/100115.htm 諏訪市博物館 - ''Suwa City Museum: Kanasa Mori-sumi'']</ref> He is also referred to as {{ill|Suwa Mori-sumi|ja|諏訪盛澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Mori-sumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺盛澄|1=Kana Moritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646497}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/gojuuon/13_su/~su_69.htm ''Suwa City Museum: Suwa Mori-sumi'']</ref> His younger brother was {{ill|Tezuka Mitsumori|ja|手塚光盛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tezuka Mitsumori|qq=}}.
## Biography Initially, Kanasa Mori-sumi joined [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] during the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] but later returned to his homeland to oversee the {{ill|Misayama Ritual|ja|御射山神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and left his younger brother Mitsumori in his stead. Due to his longstanding affiliation with the {{ill|Heike|ja|平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heike|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢平氏|1=Ise Heishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15734686}}, recorded in the ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' under the entry for [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 3 (1187), August 15, Mori-sumi was captured by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and placed under the custody of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]].
Yoritomo initially intended to execute Mori-sumi. However, Kagetoki, recognizing Mori-sumi's skill in [[:en:Kyūjutsu|archery]], persuaded Yoritomo to allow Mori-sumi to demonstrate his abilities before his execution. At the [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] during the [[:en:Life release|Hōjōe]], Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of [[:en:Yabusame|yabusame]], accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became [[:en:Gokenin|gokenin]].<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刺盛澄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11646497]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11646497]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox person | image = Suwa Morizumi.jpg | caption = Kanasa Mori-sumi (諏訪盛澄 / 金刺盛澄), depicted in the Edo-period work ''Zenkō Kojitsu'' by [[:en:Kikuchi Yōsai|Kikuchi Yōsai]]. | birth_date = 1150 | death_date = Unknown | occupation = {{ill|bushi|WD=Q1975935}} | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | children = {{ill|Yamabuki Gozen|WD=Q11467654}} | father = {{ill|Q106616045|WD=Q106616045}} }}
'''Kanasa Mori-sumi''' (金刺 盛澄, also known as Suwa Mori-sumi) was a priest ({{ill|Ōhō|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|1=Hōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869239}}) and samurai of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/1000/100115.htm 諏訪市博物館 - ''Suwa City Museum: Kanasa Mori-sumi'']</ref> He is also referred to as {{ill|Suwa Mori-sumi|ja|諏訪盛澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Mori-sumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺盛澄|1=Kana Moritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646497}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/gojuuon/13_su/~su_69.htm ''Suwa City Museum: Suwa Mori-sumi'']</ref> His younger brother was {{ill|Tezuka Mitsumori|ja|手塚光盛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tezuka Mitsumori|qq=}}.
## Biography Initially, Kanasa Mori-sumi joined [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] during the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] but later returned to his homeland to oversee the {{ill|Misayama Ritual|ja|御射山神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and left his younger brother Mitsumori in his stead. Due to his longstanding affiliation with the {{ill|Heike|ja|平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heike|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢平氏|1=Ise Heishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15734686}}, recorded in the ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' under the entry for [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 3 (1187), August 15, Mori-sumi was captured by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and placed under the custody of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]].
Yoritomo initially intended to execute Mori-sumi. However, Kagetoki, recognizing Mori-sumi's skill in [[:en:Kyūjutsu|archery]], persuaded Yoritomo to allow Mori-sumi to demonstrate his abilities before his execution. At the [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] during the [[:en:Life release|Hōjōe]], Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of [[:en:Yabusame|yabusame]], accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became [[:en:Gokenin|gokenin]].<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.
## Gallery <gallery widths="200px" heights="140px"> 梶原塚（下諏訪町）.jpg|Kajiwara Zuka (Shimosuwa Town), [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] 梶原塚全景.jpg|View of Kajiwara Zuka near Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya </gallery>

## Biography Initially, Kanasa Mori-sumi joined [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] during the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] but later returned to his homeland to oversee the {{ill|Misayama Ritual|ja|御射山神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and left his younger brother Mitsumori in his stead. Due to his longstanding affiliation with the {{ill|Heike|ja|平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heike|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢平氏|1=Ise Heishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15734686}}, recorded in the ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' under the entry for [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 3 (1187), August 15, Mori-sumi was captured by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and placed under the custody of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]].
Yoritomo initially intended to execute Mori-sumi. However, Kagetoki, recognizing Mori-sumi's skill in [[:en:Kyūjutsu|archery]], persuaded Yoritomo to allow Mori-sumi to demonstrate his abilities before his execution. At the [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] during the [[:en:Life release|Hōjōe]], Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of [[:en:Yabusame|yabusame]], accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became [[:en:Gokenin|gokenin]].<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刺盛澄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刺盛澄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刺盛澄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刺盛澄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11646497]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11646497]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox person | image = Suwa Morizumi.jpg | caption = Kanasa Mori-sumi (諏訪盛澄 / 金刺盛澄), depicted in the Edo-period work ''Zenkō Kojitsu'' by [[:en:Kikuchi Yōsai|Kikuchi Yōsai]]. | birth_date = 1150 | death_date = Unknown | occupation = {{ill|bushi|WD=Q1975935}} | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | children = {{ill|Yamabuki Gozen|WD=Q11467654}} | father = {{ill|Q106616045|WD=Q106616045}} }}
'''Kanasa Mori-sumi''' (金刺 盛澄, also known as Suwa Mori-sumi) was a priest ({{ill|Ōhō|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|1=Hōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869239}}) and samurai of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Lower Shrine during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]].<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/1000/100115.htm 諏訪市博物館 - ''Suwa City Museum: Kanasa Mori-sumi'']</ref> He is also referred to as {{ill|Suwa Mori-sumi|ja|諏訪盛澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Mori-sumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺盛澄|1=Kana Moritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646497}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/gojuuon/13_su/~su_69.htm ''Suwa City Museum: Suwa Mori-sumi'']</ref> His younger brother was {{ill|Tezuka Mitsumori|ja|手塚光盛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tezuka Mitsumori|qq=}}.
## Biography Initially, Kanasa Mori-sumi joined [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshinaka|Minamoto no Yoshinaka]] during the [[:en:Genpei War|Genpei War]] but later returned to his homeland to oversee the {{ill|Misayama Ritual|ja|御射山神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and left his younger brother Mitsumori in his stead. Due to his longstanding affiliation with the {{ill|Heike|ja|平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heike|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢平氏|1=Ise Heishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15734686}}, recorded in the ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' under the entry for [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 3 (1187), August 15, Mori-sumi was captured by [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and placed under the custody of [[:en:Kajiwara Kagetoki|Kajiwara Kagetoki]].
Yoritomo initially intended to execute Mori-sumi. However, Kagetoki, recognizing Mori-sumi's skill in [[:en:Kyūjutsu|archery]], persuaded Yoritomo to allow Mori-sumi to demonstrate his abilities before his execution. At the [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] during the [[:en:Life release|Hōjōe]], Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of [[:en:Yabusame|yabusame]], accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became [[:en:Gokenin|gokenin]].<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] and [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke Province]], where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day [[:en:Shimosuwa|Shimosuwa]], a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刺盛澄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刺盛澄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刺盛澄]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刺盛澄]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11646497]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11646497]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox person | image = Suwa Morizumi.jpg | caption = Kanasa Mori-sumi (諏訪盛澄 / 金刺盛澄), depicted in the Edo-period work ''Zenkō Kojitsu'' by {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|en|Kikuchi Yōsai|WD=Q700019|lt=Kikuchi Yōsai}}. | birth_date = 1150 | death_date = Unknown | occupation = {{ill|bushi|WD=Q1975935}} | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | children = {{ill|Yamabuki Gozen|WD=Q11467654}} | father = {{ill|Q106616045|WD=Q106616045}} }}
'''Kanasa Mori-sumi''' (金刺 盛澄, also known as Suwa Mori-sumi) was a priest ({{ill|Ōhō|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|1=Hōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869239}}) and samurai of the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} Lower Shrine during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/1000/100115.htm 諏訪市博物館 - ''Suwa City Museum: Kanasa Mori-sumi'']</ref> He is also referred to as {{ill|Suwa Mori-sumi|ja|諏訪盛澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Mori-sumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺盛澄|1=Kana Moritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646497}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/gojuuon/13_su/~su_69.htm ''Suwa City Museum: Suwa Mori-sumi'']</ref> His younger brother was {{ill|Tezuka Mitsumori|ja|手塚光盛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tezuka Mitsumori|qq=}}.
## Biography Initially, Kanasa Mori-sumi joined {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|en|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|WD=Q1139202|lt=Minamoto no Yoshinaka}} during the {{ill|Genpei War|en|Genpei War|WD=Q737542|lt=Genpei War}} but later returned to his homeland to oversee the {{ill|Misayama Ritual|ja|御射山神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and left his younger brother Mitsumori in his stead. Due to his longstanding affiliation with the {{ill|Heike|ja|平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heike|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢平氏|1=Ise Heishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15734686}}, recorded in the ''{{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|WD=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami}}'' under the entry for {{ill|Bunji (era)|en|Bunji (era)|WD=Q1194415|lt=Bunji}} 3 (1187), August 15, Mori-sumi was captured by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} and placed under the custody of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|en|Kajiwara Kagetoki|WD=Q3191855|lt=Kajiwara Kagetoki}}.
Yoritomo initially intended to execute Mori-sumi. However, Kagetoki, recognizing Mori-sumi's skill in {{ill|Kyūjutsu|en|Kyūjutsu|WD=Q11687476|lt=archery}}, persuaded Yoritomo to allow Mori-sumi to demonstrate his abilities before his execution. At the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} during the {{ill|Life release|en|Life release|WD=Q11078912|lt=Hōjōe}}, Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of {{ill|Yabusame|en|Yabusame|WD=Q1886157|lt=yabusame}}, accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became {{ill|Gokenin|en|Gokenin|WD=Q63765|lt=gokenin}}.<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|WD=Q1193933|lt=Kenkyū}} 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}} and {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province}}, where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa}}, a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.
## Gallery <gallery widths="200px" heights="140px"> 梶原塚（下諏訪町）.jpg|Kajiwara Zuka (Shimosuwa Town), {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} 梶原塚全景.jpg|View of Kajiwara Zuka near Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya </gallery>
## References * ''Nagano Encyclopedia'' (長野県百科事典), published by {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}}. * ''Dictionary of Medieval Japanese History'' (日本中世史事典), published by {{ill|Asakura Shoten|ja|朝倉書店|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Shoten|1=Asakura Shoten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11517313}}.
## See also * {{ill|Suwa Shinbō|ja|諏訪神党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shinbō|1=Suwa Shintō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631917}} * {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan}} * {{ill|Yamabuki Gozen|ja|山吹御前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabuki Gozen|1=Yamabuki Gozen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467654}} * {{ill|Orihiromitsu|ja|折井宏光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orihiromitsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Historical painter who depicted Kanasa Mori-sumi.<ref>[http://suwagunshihaku.org/article/56094157.html 諏訪湖博物館特別展: "Drawing Kanasa Mori-sumi ~ Orihiromitsu Exhibition" (2012)]</ref>

{{Infobox person | image = Suwa Morizumi.jpg | caption = Kanasa Mori-sumi (諏訪盛澄 / 金刺盛澄), depicted in the Edo-period work ''Zenkō Kojitsu'' by {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|en|Kikuchi Yōsai|WD=Q700019|lt=Kikuchi Yōsai}}. | birth_date = 1150 | death_date = Unknown | occupation = {{ill|bushi|WD=Q1975935}} | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | children = {{ill|Yamabuki Gozen|WD=Q11467654}} | father = {{ill|Q106616045|WD=Q106616045}} }}
'''Kanasa Mori-sumi''' (金刺 盛澄, also known as Suwa Mori-sumi) was a priest ({{ill|Ōhō|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|1=Hōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869239}}) and samurai of the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} Lower Shrine during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/1000/100115.htm 諏訪市博物館 - ''Suwa City Museum: Kanasa Mori-sumi'']</ref> He is also referred to as {{ill|Suwa Mori-sumi|ja|諏訪盛澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Mori-sumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺盛澄|1=Kana Moritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646497}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/gojuuon/13_su/~su_69.htm ''Suwa City Museum: Suwa Mori-sumi'']</ref> His younger brother was {{ill|Tezuka Mitsumori|ja|手塚光盛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tezuka Mitsumori|qq=}}.
## Biography Initially, Kanasa Mori-sumi joined {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|en|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|WD=Q1139202|lt=Minamoto no Yoshinaka}} during the {{ill|Genpei War|en|Genpei War|WD=Q737542|lt=Genpei War}} but later returned to his homeland to oversee the {{ill|Misayama Ritual|ja|御射山神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and left his younger brother Mitsumori in his stead. Due to his longstanding affiliation with the {{ill|Heike|ja|平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heike|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢平氏|1=Ise Heishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15734686}}, recorded in the ''{{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|WD=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami}}'' under the entry for {{ill|Bunji (era)|en|Bunji (era)|WD=Q1194415|lt=Bunji}} 3 (1187), August 15, Mori-sumi was captured by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} and placed under the custody of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|en|Kajiwara Kagetoki|WD=Q3191855|lt=Kajiwara Kagetoki}}.
Yoritomo initially intended to execute Mori-sumi. However, Kagetoki, recognizing Mori-sumi's skill in {{ill|Kyūjutsu|en|Kyūjutsu|WD=Q11687476|lt=archery}}, persuaded Yoritomo to allow Mori-sumi to demonstrate his abilities before his execution. At the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} during the {{ill|Life release|en|Life release|WD=Q11078912|lt=Hōjōe}}, Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of {{ill|Yabusame|en|Yabusame|WD=Q1886157|lt=yabusame}}, accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became {{ill|Gokenin|en|Gokenin|WD=Q63765|lt=gokenin}}.<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|WD=Q1193933|lt=Kenkyū}} 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}} and {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province}}, where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa}}, a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.
## Gallery <gallery widths="200px" heights="140px"> 梶原塚（下諏訪町）.jpg|Kajiwara Zuka (Shimosuwa Town), {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} 梶原塚全景.jpg|View of Kajiwara Zuka near Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya </gallery>

'''Kanasa Mori-sumi''' (金刺 盛澄, also known as Suwa Mori-sumi) was a priest ({{ill|Ōhō|ja|大祝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|1=Hōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869239}}) and samurai of the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} Lower Shrine during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/koumoku/1000/100115.htm 諏訪市博物館 - ''Suwa City Museum: Kanasa Mori-sumi'']</ref> He is also referred to as {{ill|Suwa Mori-sumi|ja|諏訪盛澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Mori-sumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刺盛澄|1=Kana Moritomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646497}}.<ref>[https://suwacitymuseum.jp/nandemo/gojuuon/13_su/~su_69.htm ''Suwa City Museum: Suwa Mori-sumi'']</ref> His younger brother was {{ill|Tezuka Mitsumori|ja|手塚光盛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tezuka Mitsumori|qq=}}.
## Biography Initially, Kanasa Mori-sumi joined {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|en|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|WD=Q1139202|lt=Minamoto no Yoshinaka|1=Minamoto no Yoshinaka}} during the {{ill|Genpei War|en|Genpei War|WD=Q737542|lt=Genpei War|1=Genpei War}} but later returned to his homeland to oversee the {{ill|Misayama Ritual|ja|御射山神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misayama Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and left his younger brother Mitsumori in his stead. Due to his longstanding affiliation with the {{ill|Heike|ja|平家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heike|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢平氏|1=Ise Heishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15734686}}, recorded in the ''{{ill|Azuma Kagami|en|Azuma Kagami|WD=Q1042324|lt=Azuma Kagami|1=Azuma Kagami}}'' under the entry for {{ill|Bunji (era)|en|Bunji (era)|WD=Q1194415|lt=Bunji|1=Bunji (era)}} 3 (1187), August 15, Mori-sumi was captured by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} and placed under the custody of {{ill|Kajiwara Kagetoki|en|Kajiwara Kagetoki|WD=Q3191855|lt=Kajiwara Kagetoki|1=Kajiwara Kagetoki}}.
Yoritomo initially intended to execute Mori-sumi. However, Kagetoki, recognizing Mori-sumi's skill in {{ill|Kyūjutsu|en|Kyūjutsu|WD=Q11687476|lt=archery|1=Kyūjutsu}}, persuaded Yoritomo to allow Mori-sumi to demonstrate his abilities before his execution. At the {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|1=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} during the {{ill|Life release|en|Life release|WD=Q11078912|lt=Hōjōe|1=Life release}}, Mori-sumi performed an exceptional feat of {{ill|Yabusame|en|Yabusame|WD=Q1886157|lt=yabusame|1=Yabusame}}, accurately shooting eight targets, their fragments, and the stands holding them. Impressed, Yoritomo pardoned him. Following Kagetoki's further petitions, several of Yoshinaka's retainers were also spared and became {{ill|Gokenin|en|Gokenin|WD=Q63765|lt=gokenin|1=Gokenin}}.<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%AB%8F%E8%A8%AA%E7%9B%9B%E6%BE%84-1084644 ''Suwa Mori-sumi''] - Kotobank</ref>
In {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|WD=Q1193933|lt=Kenkyū|1=Kenkyū}} 4 (1193), Mori-sumi accompanied Yoritomo on hunts in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}} and {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}}, where he continued to serve at ceremonial events involving archery. By 1203, his activities are still noted in historical records. In modern-day {{ill|Shimosuwa|en|Shimosuwa|WD=Q1204211|lt=Shimosuwa|1=Shimosuwa}}, a mound called "Kajiwara Zuka" commemorates Mori-sumi's gratitude to Kagetoki.
## Gallery <gallery widths="200px" heights="140px"> 梶原塚（下諏訪町）.jpg|Kajiwara Zuka (Shimosuwa Town), {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} 梶原塚全景.jpg|View of Kajiwara Zuka near Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya </gallery>
